---
title: ""
format: 
  revealjs: 
    slide-level: 3
    link-external-newwindow: true
    chalkboard: true
    controls: true
    auto-stretch: false
    code-overflow: wrap
tbl-colwidths: [70,25]
---

## Brain Exam 2 Review! {.smaller}

[Check-In Here](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSdcpCbJT1Q-PGcOMwBPuhodSX2CsBsTb9ORh0myBxplztkO2w/viewform?usp=header)

::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
d <- read.csv("~/Dropbox/!WHY STATS/Professor Datasets/Culture and Awe (2024)/culture_awe_fear.csv", stringsAsFactors = T)
mod <- lm(Fear ~ Anger, data = d)
summary(mod)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
1.  Describe the relationship between these two variables.
2.  What is the standard error of the slope, and what does this statistic describe?
3.  What is the t-value for the slope, and what does this statistic describe?
4.  What is the p-value for the slope, and what does this statistic describe?
5.  Is the slope considered large? How do you know??
:::
:::::

## Check-In Review{.smaller}

::: panel-tabset

### Q1

::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
d <- read.csv("~/Dropbox/!WHY STATS/Professor Datasets/Culture and Awe (2024)/culture_awe_fear.csv", stringsAsFactors = T)
mod <- lm(Fear ~ Anger, data = d)
summary(mod)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
1.  Describe the relationship between these two variables.

:::
:::::

### Q2

::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
d <- read.csv("~/Dropbox/!WHY STATS/Professor Datasets/Culture and Awe (2024)/culture_awe_fear.csv", stringsAsFactors = T)
mod <- lm(Fear ~ Anger, data = d)
summary(mod)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
2.  What is the standard error of the slope, and what does this statistic describe?

:::
:::::

### Q3

::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
d <- read.csv("~/Dropbox/!WHY STATS/Professor Datasets/Culture and Awe (2024)/culture_awe_fear.csv", stringsAsFactors = T)
mod <- lm(Fear ~ Anger, data = d)
summary(mod)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
3.  What is the t-value for the slope, and what does this statistic describe?

:::
:::::

### Q4

::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
d <- read.csv("~/Dropbox/!WHY STATS/Professor Datasets/Culture and Awe (2024)/culture_awe_fear.csv", stringsAsFactors = T)
mod <- lm(Fear ~ Anger, data = d)
summary(mod)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}
4.  What is the p-value for the slope, and what does this statistic describe?

:::
:::::

### Q5

::::: columns
::: {.column width="55%"}
```{r}
d <- read.csv("~/Dropbox/!WHY STATS/Professor Datasets/Culture and Awe (2024)/culture_awe_fear.csv", stringsAsFactors = T)
mod <- lm(Fear ~ Anger, data = d)
summary(mod)
```
:::

::: {.column width="45%"}

5.  Is the slope considered large? How do you know??
:::
:::::


:::

## Project Time {.smaller}

![](images/clipboard-3096593119.png){fig-align="center" width="80%"}

## [Milestone 3](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ScvG86PiyyqHO7Ob5bki04XGzCwLodQ4h57n9Jq5DYI/edit?tab=t.0#bookmark=kix.tscgdcazxbbz)
